Definition of Done: Scope doc exists in repo with explicit in-scope and out-of-scope items, and it is accepted.

Issue Title: Freeze core business actors and roles
Definition of Done: Actors are documented (customer, staff, admin, notification provider) and staff vs admin permissions are defined.
Official order statuses and allowed/invalid transitions are documented and treated as source of truth.
Official product states (Draft, Published) and publish/unpublish rules are documented.
Immediate stock reservation/reduction, cancellation restore, and no-negative-stock rules are documented.
Notification-triggering events, MVP-first channel, and non-rollback failure rule are documented.

Issue Title: Create BDD folder structure in the repo
Definition of Done: docs/bdd/mvp/ exists with all expected subfolders and a short README.

Issue Title: Add the full MVP feature-file pack to the repo
Definition of Done: MVP .feature files are committed for auth, catalog, orders, products, inventory, notifications, and platform.
Statuses, actor names, and tags are consistent across all feature files.
Core business terms are defined and aligned with BDD wording.
Non-negotiable rules are documented clearly enough to convert into tests later.
ARCHITECTURE.md exists and documents boundaries between apps and packages.
Database and ORM/query layer are finalized and documented for MVP.
Core tables, relations, required fields, and nullability are defined.
Fresh local setup can create the schema from migrations successfully.
Seed script creates usable local admin, product, variant, and sample order data.
Contract folders exist for auth, products, inventory, orders, notifications, and health.

Issue Title: Define auth request and response contracts
Definition of Done: Typed contracts exist for login, logout, current user, request reset, and confirm reset.

Issue Title: Define public catalog and product contracts
Definition of Done: Typed contracts exist for public product listing and product detail with only public-safe fields.
Typed contracts exist for create order, lookup order, admin order list/detail, and status updates.

Issue Title: Define standard error and pagination contracts
Definition of Done: Shared API error and pagination shapes are defined and reusable.
Nest modules exist for health, auth, products, inventory, orders, and notifications.

Issue Title: Add global validation and error handling
Definition of Done: Invalid requests return stable structured errors and validation is enforced globally.
Required env vars are centrally loaded, validated, and fail fast when missing.
GET /health returns 200, is public, and has at least one API test.

Issue Title: Implement admin user persistence and password hashing
Definition of Done: Admin users are stored correctly and passwords are hashed and verifiable.
Valid credentials create a session, invalid ones fail generically, disabled users are rejected.
Authenticated sessions persist correctly and logout invalidates access.
Protected routes reject unauthenticated users and allow valid authenticated users.
admin and staff roles are enforced correctly on protected routes.
Auth tests cover login success/failure, logout, guards, and role restrictions.
Back-office users can create, update, and list products successfully.
Products support multiple valid variants with required MVP fields.
Incomplete products cannot be published; unpublished products are not public.
Public listing returns only published products and supports category filtering.
Public detail returns name, description, variants, media/availability, and blocks unpublished products.
Tests cover public visibility, filtering, publish validation, and detail loading.
Back office can increase/decrease stock and negative stock is blocked.
Tests cover stock increase, decrease, negative-stock prevention, and adjustment behavior.
Guests can create orders with required fields and invalid requests are rejected cleanly.

Issue Title: Implement stock reservation on order creation
Definition of Done: Order creation updates stock atomically and rejects insufficient stock.
Each order gets a unique readable reference usable in customer flows.
Orders preserve product/variant/price snapshot data even if catalog data changes later.
Customers can look up orders via reference + phone without leaking other order data.

Issue Title: Add order placement and lookup tests
Definition of Done: Tests cover successful order creation, validation failures, stock failures, and lookup success/failure.
